political.s: <cite index="1-2,1-3">In the 2024 cycle, Best Buy PAC contributions totaled $450,728 with lobbying spending of $1,360,000.</cite> The FEC data shows bipartisan distribution with 28% to Republicans and 54% to Democrats. <cite index="9-1">Best Buy discontinued contributions to the 147 Congressional members who voted against certifying the 2020 presidential election results.</cite>

charity.s: <cite index="13-1">The Best Buy Foundation supports educational and career opportunities for teens through initiatives like the Best Buy Teen Tech Center program, and promotes vibrant communities through grants to nonprofit organizations across the country.</cite> <cite index="14-4,14-6">The Best Buy Foundation is described as a refreshingly transparent corporate funder and reliable source of support for community organizations of any size that provide out-of-school technology or career education to teenagers.</cite>
charity.focus: Technology education, youth development, community support

environment.s: <cite index="25-5">Best Buy achieved an 'A' rating from CDP out of a record-breaking 23,000 companies who responded to the 2023 Climate Change questionnaire.</cite> <cite index="23-2">The company reduced carbon emissions 69% over a 2009 baseline, compared to its goal of becoming carbon neutral by 2040.</cite> <cite index="29-1">Nearly 69% of supply chain facilities achieved zero waste TRUE certification.</cite>

labor.s: Best Buy has engaged in union organizing challenges with employee organizing efforts documented. <cite index="31-9,31-10">The company's arbitration policy requires that covered claims be arbitrated on an individual basis, with parties waiving rights to class or collective actions.</cite> Limited public data on OSHA violations or wage issues, though historical litigation shows varied outcomes on employee compensation claims.

dei.s: <cite index="41-1,41-4">Best Buy removed key diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) commitments from its 2024 Corporate Responsibility & Sustainability Report, with measurable DEI hiring goals replaced by broad statements about fostering inclusion.</cite> <cite index="50-2,50-3">In 2025, Best Buy shareholders voted to reject an anti-DEI proposal asking the company to end its relationship with the Human Rights Campaign's Corporate Equality Index.</cite>

animals.s: Best Buy is a consumer electronics retailer with no animal testing or animal-related products in its core business.

guns.s: Best Buy sells firearms and ammunition in select locations as part of its consumer goods retail operations.

privacy.s: <cite index="51-8,51-9">Best Buy states that customer privacy is a major priority and that the Best Buy Privacy Policy lays out the ways in which the company gathers and protects information, and how customers can control their personal information.</cite> <cite index="53-5,53-6">Best Buy's privacy policy appears focused on protecting customer privacy and providing transparency regarding data collection and use practices, with measures to protect personal information and customer management of preferences.</cite> However, <cite index="60-1">the company uses facial recognition from security cameras to protect health and safety and prevent criminal activities.</cite>

execPay.s: <cite index="63-1">CEO Corie Barry received $16.15M in total compensation in 2025.</cite> <cite index="68-9">Barry's pay is estimated to be 339–429 times higher than that of a line-level employee.</cite> <cite index="70-10,70-11">Her compensation of $16.15M is about average for companies of similar size in the US market, and has been consistent with company performance over the past year.</cite>

fec.committeeName: BEST BUY CO., INC. EMPLOYEE POLITICAL FORUM

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
